+"""This module defines abstractions related to instrument channels."""
+import abc
+
+import numpy as np
+import xarray
+from scipy.interpolate import interp1d
+
+import astropy.units as u
+
+__all__ = ["AbstractChannel", "SourceSpectra"]
+
+
+class SourceSpectra:
+    def __init__(self, temperature, wavelength, spectra, density=None, **kwargs):
+        # Internal xarray representation
+        coords = {
+            "temperature": xarray.Variable(
+                "temperature",
+                temperature.value,
+                attrs={"unit": temperature.unit.to_string()},
+            ),
+            "wavelength": xarray.Variable(
+                "wavelength",
+                wavelength.value,
+                attrs={"unit": wavelength.unit.to_string()},
+            ),
+        }
+        if density is None:
+            coords["density"] = xarray.Variable(
+                "temperature", density.value, attrs={"unit": density.unit.to_string()}
+            )
+        self._da = xarray.DataArray(
+            spectra.data,
+            dims=["temperature", "wavelength"],
+            coords=coords,
+            attrs={"unit": spectra.unit.to_string(), **kwargs},
+        )
+
+    @property
+    def temperature(self) -> u.K:
+        return u.Quantity(self._da.temperature.data, self._da.temperature.attrs["unit"])
+
+    @property
+    def wavelength(self) -> u.Angstrom:
+        return u.Quantity(self._da.wavelength.data, self._da.wavelength.attrs["unit"])
+
+    @property
+    def data(self) -> u.photon * u.cm**3 / (u.s * u.AA * u.sr):
+        return u.Quantity(self._da.data, self._da.attrs["unit"])
+
+
+class AbstractChannel(abc.ABC):
+    """An abstract base class for defining instrument channels.
+
+    .. caution::
+
+       This abstract class is still under development and may change
+       in the near future.
+    """
+
+    def temperature_response(
+        self, source_spectra, obstime=None, **kwargs
+    ) -> u.cm**5 * u.DN / (u.pix * u.s):
+        wave_response = self.wavelength_response(obstime=obstime, **kwargs)
+        f_response = interp1d(
+            self.wavelength.to_value("AA"),
+            wave_response.to_value(),
+            axis=0,
+            bounds_error=False,
+            fill_value=0.0,
+        )
+        wave_response_interp = u.Quantity(
+            f_response(source_spectra.wavelength.to_value("AA")), wave_response.unit
+        )
+        temperature_response = (
+            source_spectra * wave_response_interp * np.gradient(self.wavelength)
+        ).sum(axis=1)
+        return source_spectra.temperature, temperature_response
+
+    def wavelength_response(
+        self, obstime=None, **kwargs
+    ) -> u.cm**2 * u.DN * u.steradian / (u.photon * u.pixel):
+        area_eff = self.effective_area(obstime=obstime, **kwargs)
+        return (
+            area_eff
+            * self.photon_to_energy
+            * self.pixel_area
+            / (self.energy_to_electron * self.camera_gain)
+        )
+
+    def effective_area(self, obstime=None) -> u.cm**2:
+        return (
+            self.geometrical_area
+            * self.mirror_reflectance
+            * self.filter_transmittance
+            * self.quantum_efficiency
+            * self.degradation(obstime=obstime)
+        )
+
+    @property
+    def photon_to_energy(self) -> u.eV / u.photon:
+        return self.wavelength.to("eV", equivalencies=u.spectral()) / u.photon
+
+    @abc.abstractmethod
+    def degradation(self, obstime=None):

When opening a fits file like:
hdu = fits.open(filename, checksum=True)
is supposed to report an error is the checksum isn't correct. The behavior when opening a file that way when the header doesn't contain a checksum is not specified in the documentation, but it seems to me that an error should be reported in that case. Otherwise the behavior seems to imply that the checksum was present and correct. There doesn't seem to be a way to verify that there is a checksum is present within the call. (Of course one could do something like 'checksum' in hdr.)

### Expected behavior

I would expect some sort of exception or maybe an AstropyUserWarning to be issued.

### How to Reproduce

```python
# Put your Python code snippet here.
from astropy.io import fits
file = 'test_file.fits' # fits file that lacks a checksum
hdu = fits.open(file, checksum=True)
```

Description

When opening a fits file like:
```python
hdu = fits.open(filename, checksum=True)
```
is supposed to report an error is the checksum isn't correct. The behavior when opening a file that way when the header doesn't contain a checksum is not specified in the documentation, but it seems to me that an error should be reported in that case. Otherwise the behavior seems to imply that the checksum was present and correct. There doesn't seem to be a way to verify that there is a checksum is present within the call. (Of course one could do something like 'checksum' in hdr.)

### Expected behavior

I would expect some sort of exception or maybe an AstropyUserWarning to be issued.

### How to Reproduce

```python
# Put your Python code snippet here.
from astropy.io import fits
file = 'test_file.fits' # fits file that lacks a checksum
hdu = fits.open(file, checksum=True)
```

HISTORY XRT_SPOTCOR v2014-Apr-14: (23-May-2015 00:52:36) All spots and dust     
HISTORY (cont'd) corrected with thin plate splines. Spot mastermap version =    
HISTORY (cont'd) v20080206. Threshold = 0.500000

HISTORY gin(cont'd) file: XRT20120605_215839.9.fitsLEVEL1_PREP v2015.May.19 NOTE
HISTORY : No spot correction done for this image;(cont'd) XRT_SPOTCOR informatio
HISTORY n applies to associated jpeg files onlyXRT_SPOTCOR v2014-Apr-14: (23-May
HISTORY -2015 00:52:36) All spots and dust(cont'd) corrected with thin plate spl
HISTORY ines. Spot mastermap version =(cont'd) v20080206. Threshold = 0.500000XR

### Proposed solution

I'm not sure where or how SunPy joins the lines in the HISTORY metadata, but it seems that the line breaks are somehow removed at that point. So, it may be as easy as not removing the line breaks. I looked through the code a bit and didn't see any place where HISTORY metadata is treated differently than any other data in the map code. In astropy there is a separate type of header keyword called a commentary_keyword. Provide a general description of the issue or problem.

I'm submitting this issue at the request of Will Barnes. It's not clear to me whether it would be considered a feature request or a bug.

The issue is that if you have a masked image in a Map and apply the superpixel method, the resulting image will only be masked for pixels for which all the original pixels of the superpixel are masked. This occurs because superpixel treats the masked pixels as 0s and does not sum the mask itself. A way around that would be to sum the mask as well, which then leads to all the superpixels being masked in which any of the original pixels are masked. One could debate the proper behavior in this case, though it's clear that proper summing, for example, is not being done if some of the pixels are being masked.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/6769/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/6769/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/comments/1419392787","html_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/issues/6769#issuecomment-1419392787","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/6769","id":1419392787,"node_id":"IC_kwDOACEKh85UmjcT","user":{"login":"jslavin","id":644963,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjY0NDk2Mw==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/644963?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jslavin","html_url":"https://github.com/jslavin","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jslavin/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jslavin/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jslavin/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jslavin/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jslavin/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jslavin/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jslavin/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jslavin/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jslavin/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-02-06T16:45:20Z","updated_at":"2023-02-06T16:45:20Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"For my particular application it is a bug, so I'd agree with you.
